chunk_text:
These are the documents you will upload when you log in to Compass to submit your application for the Post-Completion OPT I-20.

| 1. | OPT Academic Adviser Formto be completed by you and your Department Administrator. Undergraduate students in CC/SEAS should contact the Berick Center. GS students should contact their advising dean at the Dean of Students Office. Some schools/programs submit a list of all prospective graduating students to the ISSO. The OPT Academic Adviser Form is not needed for:Business School (MBA - May Graduates only & MS Business Programs)Climate SchoolColumbia Journalism School (MA and MS Programs)GSAPPGSAS (MA Mathematics of Finance, MA QMSS, & MA Statistics only)Law SchoolSchool of Social Work (MS Social Work)SIPA |

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: Gather Documents and Information for ISSO Application > Use Compass to Submit Your ISSO Application
chunk_text:
**The earliest you can submit your application** to the ISSO for your OPT I-20 is 90 days before your [program end date](#text-15952)[.](#/text-15952)

*Please note*: If you submit your application to ISSO in less than 10 business days prior to your filing deadline with USCIS, ISSO cannot guarantee your I-20 will be processed in time.

Click the appropriate link below to log in to Compass:

**Not sure of your campus? **Check our [campus school list](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/schools-columbia-campuses).**Need help using Compass? **Learn more on our [Compass User Guide](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/compass-user-guide).

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: We Will Contact You
chunk_text:
We will send a message to your Columbia email address when your digital OPT I-20 has been processed, which you will need for your USCIS application. Please note that 10 business days is the maximum processing time, not the average, and we will process your request as quickly as possible.

**Receive** Your OPT I-20

- You are required to review the I-20 and verify all information is correct, including the OPT requested start and end dates. Your OPT recommendation is notated on page two. Then, you need to
**print and sign**the attached digital I-20 upon receipt. - You must submit your
**I-765**application within 30 days after the date this OPT I-20 was issued or it will be denied. Note: USCIS follows UTC (Coordinated Universal Time). - Always use your most recent I-20 when you travel. Be aware that the travel signature on page 2 is valid for six months (not 12) when you’re on OPT.
- Keep all previously issued I-20s. You may need them for future applications.

**Submit your OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account.**

After you have your OPT I-20, the online application is easy to use. Watch this [short video](https://youtu.be/wksu9QMh_TI) on the many advantages of filing online as compared to mailing a paper application. ISSO continues to highly recommend that you file online.

The ISSO provides the following tips regarding the OPT application, however, please keep in mind that** **you are responsible for the accuracy of your application to USCIS and following their step-by-step instructions.

While not required for a successful application, USCIS offers premium processing ([read the details](https://www.uscis.gov/forms/all-forms/how-do-i-request-premium-processing)).

chunk_text:
- Copy of your most recent
[I-94 arrival](https://i94.cbp.dhs.gov/I94/#/recent-search)record (not your travel history). Make sure the record indicates “Class of Admission: F1" and “Admit Until Date: D/S,” which stands for duration of status.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: We Will Contact You > “Previously Authorized CPT or OPT”
chunk_text:
- For CPT: Upload copy of your CPT I-20(s).
- For OPT: Upload copy of your EAD card(s).
- If you are missing I-20(s) or EAD card(s), upload a letter (template
[here](https://isso.columbia.edu/sites/default/files/content/sampleletters/Lost_I20s_sample_letter.docx)).

If you have never been previously authorized for CPT or OPT, then uploading documentation is not applicable or required.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: We Will Contact You > “Application Fee”
chunk_text: - Pay the required application fee. Remember that once you pay the fee, there can be no changes to your application as it will be considered submitted.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: We Will Contact You > “Review and Submit”
chunk_text: - Check that you are within the 30-day window after the date your Post-Completion OPT I-20 was issued. USCIS uses Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), which is several hours ahead of U.S. time, depending on the time zone. ISSO recommends that you plan ahead and submit your application to USCIS at least 1-2 days before the end of your filing window. Do not submit if it has been 28 days or more since your Post-Completion OPT I-20 was issued. In this case, contact ISSO for an updated OPT recommendation on the I-20.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: After You Submit Your OPT Application
chunk_text:
Follow USCIS guidelines for [tracking EAD card delivery](https://www.uscis.gov/forms/filing-guidance/how-to-track-delivery-of-your-green-card-employment-authorization-document-ead-and-travel-document). Monitor your application status via your USCIS account and sign up for [USPS informed delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/box/pages/intro/start.action) to track the delivery of your documents.

**Normally, you will receive 2 updates from USCIS before the EAD. **You do not need to contact us when you receive these two updates. If you receive other communication that requires follow up, contact the ISSO for assistance.

1. I-797 Receipt Notice with a case number. You will receive a case number immediately after submitting the online application.Check for accuracy in case there’s an error in your name or other information that needs to be corrected.

2. I-797 Approval Notice

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > How long will my application take?
chunk_text:
The USCIS processing time of OPT applications can vary. Check [USCIS Case Processing Times](https://egov.uscis.gov/processing-times/) to find out how long I-765 applications are currently taking.

If your case has been pending outside USCIS's listed case processing time for I-765 applications, submit an inquiry to USCIS via your online account.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > What do I tell my employer if they want me to start working as soon as possible?
chunk_text: Unfortunately, it is not possible to know exactly when your OPT application will be approved, and you need the EAD in hand to begin employment. You may need to be flexible in regard to your OPT start date.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > Can I work without pay while waiting for my EAD card?
chunk_text: Any activity that could be considered OPT employment cannot begin until you have your EAD card and are within the approved dates on the EAD card. You cannot work, paid or unpaid, for an employer until your EAD card arrives. Still job-hunting? [Review the types of employment that are allowed for OPT.](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/f-1-opt-optional-practical-training-after-your-degree-program#/cu_accordion_item-11490)

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > How long can I stay in the U.S. while my OPT application is pending?
chunk_text: The 60-day grace period no longer applies once you have applied for OPT before the deadline. You are able to stay in the US for as long as it takes USCIS to adjudicate your request.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > What happens if my OPT is approved after the start date I requested on my I-20?
chunk_text:
Generally, USCIS will adjust the start date on your EAD card to the date that USCIS approved your OPT.

Regardless of when you are approved, F-1 regulations limit the extension of your OPT end date to 14 months after your program end date.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > Can I travel while my OPT application is pending?
chunk_text: Travel before OPT approval is not recommended. If you decide you must travel, [check here for the risks involved and the required documents](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/travel-during-f-1-opt#/cu_accordion_item-1318).

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > What if USCIS case status indicates that my EAD card is delivered but I did not receive it?
chunk_text:
Try one of these two methods:

- Use USPS’
[Missing Mail and Lost Package Form](https://www.usps.com/help/missing-mail.htm)to locate your envelope.

- Visit your local US Post Office and ask them for detailed scanning information about where and when your package was scanned.

If USPS lost the EAD, attach a letter from the US Postal Service on official letterhead, stating that the USPS is responsible for the lost EAD, including the contact person from the US Post Office to send an email to the ISSO.

- Morningside: isso@columbia.edu
- CUIMC: cumc-intl-students@columbia.edu

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > What if I receive a biometrics request from USCIS?
chunk_text: Applicants filing Form I-765 for employment authorization such as OPT and STEM OPT may receive a notice requiring them to attend a Biometrics Services Appointment at an [ASC](https://www.uscis.gov/about-us/find-a-uscis-office/application-support-centers). The notice with the appointment date and time will be uploaded to your USCIS account. Communication from USCIS regarding your application may also come to you via email or physical mail,mailed to the address listed on the I-765 application. **Frequently monitor your USCIS account for communication that requires follow up.**

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > How to prepare for a biometrics appointment?
chunk_text: Please carefully review your USCIS notice for instructions, location, date and time and documents to bring with you to the appointment. For additional information, please visit [USCIS ](https://www.uscis.gov/forms/filing-guidance/preparing-for-your-biometric-services-appointment)website.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: USCIS Processing Time and EAD Delivery FAQs > What happens after a biometrics appointment?
chunk_text: Check your [USCIS Account](https://my.uscis.gov/) for any updates after your visit.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: After You Receive Your EAD > Make a copy (front and back) of your EAD
chunk_text: You should have already made a copy of your application materials before submitting your application to USCIS. Now add a copy of the EAD. You may need them for future immigration or visa applications.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: After You Receive Your EAD > Apply for a Social Security Number (if you don't have one)
chunk_text:
If you do not already have an SSN, and didn't apply for one when you completed the I-765 OPT application, you are [now eligible to apply](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/social-security-number-application) on or after the start date on your EAD card.

Do not apply again if you have already done so.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: After You Receive Your EAD > Reporting Your OPT
chunk_text: Once you are on OPT, report employment and any other changes within 10 days. [Review the types of employment (paid and unpaid) that are allowed](#cu_accordion_item-15915). Visit our [Reporting Requirements page](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/opt-reporting) to learn more.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: After You Receive Your EAD > Extending Your OPT
chunk_text:
In general, post-completion OPT cannot be extended, except in the following two situations:

- STEM OPT Extension: If you graduated from a degree program in a government-designated STEM field, you may be eligible for an extension for an additional 24 months of OPT. Read more about the
[STEM OPT extension](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/f-1-stem-opt-extension).

- Cap-Gap Extension: If your employer files a change of status H-1B petition while you are on authorized OPT and it is selected by USCIS for processing, your OPT may be automatically extended during the period of time between the expiration of your OPT and the October 1st change of status to H-1B. Read more about the “
[Cap Gap](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/cap-gap-opt-extension)” extension.

Learn more! Watch our [Extending Your OPT video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=B55d5nEMYjM&index=10&list=PLZ86wGfKZc1Qv7ScKF8Bs9Fev5mlZvkvD) for more information.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: After You Receive Your EAD > Traveling During OPT
chunk_text:
While you are on OPT, you remain in F-1 status. If you have plans to travel abroad during this period, you need to know in advance which F-1 documents you need to have with you to return to the U.S. so you won't jeopardize your OPT authorization.

Do not come back to the U.S. in any status other than F-1! Visit our [Travel During F-1 OPT](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/travel-during-f-1-opt) page for complete information. Our [Travel Abroad on OPT](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fQ9jRATHyQA&list=PLZ86wGfKZc1Qv7ScKF8Bs9Fev5mlZvkvD&index=9) video gives a great overview.

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: After You Receive Your EAD > Taking Classes During OPT
chunk_text:
During OPT, you are not permitted to study toward a degree program. Study is limited to part-time non-degree classes.

If you decide to begin a new full-time program at:

| ✓ | Columbia: Follow the instructions atBeginning a New Program. Your OPT is no longer valid when you get a new I-20 for the new program even if your EAD card has not expired. |
| ✓ | another U.S. school: Follow our "Transfer Out" instructions to have ISSO transfer your SEVIS record to the new school to obtain a new I-20 from them. As soon as you transfer your SEVIS record to the new program, the remainder of your OPT is forfeited and you must stop working. |

title: F-1 OPT (Optional Practical Training) After Your Degree Program | ISSO
section: After You Receive Your EAD > Expiration of Your EAD
chunk_text:
When your EAD expires, you have a 60 day grace period in which:

| ✓ | to remain in the U.S. to prepare for departure or travel domestically, or |
| ✓ | transfer to another school, or |
| ✓ | change your visa status |

You cannot work during the grace period, or leave and return to the U.S. in F-1 status. On the 61st day after the EAD expires, SEVIS automatically completes your F-1 SEVIS record.

If you decide to discontinue your OPT before the EAD expires, you do not have a grace period and must report your departure through ** OPT Reporting. ** This will complete your F-1 SEVIS record.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: What is the STEM OPT Extension?
chunk_text: The STEM OPT Extension is a 24-month extension of OPT (Optional Practical Training) that is available to students in F-1 status who completed a degree program in a government-approved list of STEM fields. The STEM OPT extension begins the day after the Post-Completion OPT EAD expires.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: What is the STEM OPT Extension? > STEM OPT Canvas Course
chunk_text:
We know planning for STEM OPT is not easy. To help ease this stress and better prepare you for completing a successful application, the ISSO has designed an online course exclusively for you. This course will give you the tools and resources you need to successfully complete your application for work authorization.

This online, self-paced Canvas course is required for any F-1 student applying for STEM OPT. At the end of the course, remember to download and save your certificate of completion. You will need this PDF when applying for your STEM OPT I-20. Your course completion certificate must be dated within 6 months of the submission of your STEM I-20 request to the ISSO.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Eligibility
chunk_text:
Read this chart to see if you are eligible for a OPT STEM Extension.

| ✓ | You currently must be on post-completion OPT, working for a U.S. employer in a job directly related to your major field of study. Keep in mind that STEM OPT employment must be paid; volunteering does not count toward the minimum of 20 hours/week employment. |
| ✓ | You must have reported OPT employment using theonline report form |
| ✓ | Have not had more than 90 days of unemployment during post-completion OPT |
| ✓ | You successfully completed a Bachelor’s, Master’s, or doctoral degree in a field on thegovernment's STEM CIP Code List. To check if your degree program is eligible, look at page 1 of your I-20. The code is listed next to your Program Major. |
| ✓ | You must have a job or job offer for at least 20 hours/week paid employment from an employer registered with theE-Verifyemployment verification system |
| ✓ | You can not be self-employed |
| ✓ | You may not use STEM OPT more than two times |

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Before You Apply > You must be in the U.S. at the time you submit your STEM OPT application to USCIS
chunk_text: F-1 regulations require you to be in the U.S. in F-1 status at the time you apply for any type of OPT. Your most recent I-94 admission record is among the required documentation.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Before You Apply > You must apply on time
chunk_text:
- The earliest you may submit an application to the ISSO for your OPT STEM Extension I-20 is 90 days before the end date of your initial period of Post-Completion OPT. Please allow up to
**10 business days**for processing. - The latest USCIS can receive
your OPT STEM Extension application is the date your EAD expires, but don't wait until the last minute.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Before You Apply > You have a limited number of days without employment
chunk_text:
- You are ineligible for a STEM OPT extension if you exceed 90 days without employment during post-completion OPT.

- You are limited to a total of 150 days without employment during the period encompassing both post-completion OPT and the STEM OPT extension.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Before You Apply > Example
chunk_text:
For example, if you had 90 days of unemployment during post-completion OPT, that leaves you with a maximum of 60 days allowed without employment during the 24 months of the STEM OPT extension.

If, however, you had accrued 30 days without work during post-completion OPT, you would have up to 120 days during the STEM OPT extension period.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Before You Apply > Your employer has requirements and responsibilities
chunk_text:
In addition to being enrolled in E-Verify, your STEM OPT employer:

- has to abide by certain terms and conditions for participation
- must provide appropriate training
- must maintain a bona fide employer-employee relationship with you
- has reporting obligations to the ISSO. Learn more at our
[STEM OPT Reporting section](#/cu_accordion_item-16291)below.

STEM OPT may take place at a site other than the employer’s principal place of business as long as all of the training obligations are met. Staffing and temporary agencies and consulting firms may seek to employ students under the STEM OPT program, but only if they will be the entity that provides the practical training experience to the student and they have and maintain a bona fide employer-employee relationship with the student.

Learn more about this and [employer responsibilities from USCIS](https://www.uscis.gov/working-united-states/students-and-exchange-visitors/students-and-employment/stem-opt).

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Before You Apply > Remote Work
chunk_text:
It is not explicitly prohibited to work remotely on the STEM OPT Extension provided you and your employer continue to maintain all [ISSO reporting](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/opt-reporting) and [USCIS STEM requirements](https://www.uscis.gov/working-united-states/students-and-exchange-visitors/students-and-employment/stem-opt), however, it is not recommended by the ISSO.

Per F-1 regulations, all employers must be able to verify a bona fide relationship with their employee (and vice-versa) and be ready for site visit(s) if deemed required. As such, we are unable to authorize remote work and the determination of if it is acceptable would be at the discretion of you and your employer as well as potentially reviewed by USCIS.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Pay the STEM OPT Administration Fee
chunk_text:
Pay the [$650 STEM OPT Administration Fee](https://forms.isso.columbia.edu/trainee). You remain in F-1 status under Columbia’s sponsorship with an active SEVIS record during your authorized period of 24 months of a STEM OPT extension.

After payment, you will receive an email from Cybersource with your STEM OPT Administration Fee receipt.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Gather Your Documents and Information
chunk_text: These are the documents you will upload when you login to Compass to submit your application for the STEM OPT Extension I-20.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Gather Your Documents and Information > ISSO Document and Information Checklist
chunk_text:
| 1. | Use oursample formto complete theForm I-983 Training Plan. The form must be completed and signed by you and your employer. Do not upload an editable PDF to the ISSO. You must upload a scanned version of the hard copy document. |
| 2. | Copy of your most recentI-94 Arrival Record(not your travel history), which is evidence of F-1 status. Make sure the record indicates you were admitted until "D/S." |
| 3. | Copy of front and back of currentPost-Completion OPT EAD. |
| 4. | PriorSTEM Degree Certification Form(onlyif the degree was earned at another school). |
| 5. | The ISSO requires you to take theSTEM OPT Canvas Courseand upload the certification of course completion. |

**Use Compass to Submit Your ISSO Application**

The earliest you can submit your application to the ISSO for your STEM OPT Extension I-20 is 90 days before your OPT expires.

*Please note*: If you submit your application to ISSO in less than 10 business days prior to your filing deadline with USCIS, ISSO cannot guarantee your I-20 will be processed in time.

Please click the appropriate link below:

| ISSO Morningside | ISSO-CUIMC |
| F-1 Students | F-1 Students |

**Not sure of your campus? **Check our [campus school list](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/schools-columbia-campuses).**Need help using Compass? **Learn more on our [Compass User Guide](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/compass-user-guide).

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: We Will Contact You
chunk_text: We will send a message to your Columbia email address when your digital STEM OPT I-20 has been processed, which you will need for your USCIS application. Please note that 10 business days is the maximum processing time, not the average, and we will process your request as quickly as possible.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Receive Your STEM OPT I-20
chunk_text:
- Review your new I-20 for accuracy, including the CIP code next to your Progam Major on page one. Review page 2 to verify the STEM OPT dates, then print and sign page 1.
- You must submit your application
**within 60 days after the date this STEM OPT I-20 was issued**and before the expiration of your current EAD or it will be denied. - Always use your most recent I-20 when you travel. Be aware that the travel signature on page 2 is valid for six months (not 12) when you’re on OPT.
- Keep all previously issued I-20s. You may need them for future applications.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account.
chunk_text:
After you have your STEM OPT I-20, the online application is easy to use. Watch this [short video](https://youtu.be/wksu9QMh_TI) on the many advantages of filing online as compared to mailing a paper application. ISSO continues to highly recommend that you file online.

The ISSO provides the following tips regarding the OPT application, however, please keep in mind that you are responsible for the accuracy of your application to USCIS and following their step-by-step instructions.

While not required for a successful application, USCIS offers premium processing.

If filing close to your deadline, please note that USCIS uses Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), which is several hours ahead of U.S. time, depending on the time zone. ISSO recommends that you plan ahead and submit your application to USCIS at least 1-2 days before the end of your filing window.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > 1Create or Log in to Your USCIS Account
chunk_text:
- Follow instructions for
[creating a USCIS Online Account](https://www.uscis.gov/file-online/how-to-create-a-uscis-online-account), or log in to your USCIS account if you already have one.

- Select “
[File a Form Online](http://uscis.gov/file-online)” and choose “Application for Employment Authorization (I-765)”.

- Follow the instructions and complete each step in the order they are presented to you.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “What is your eligibility category?”
chunk_text: - Choose “(c)(3)(c) Student STEM Extension.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > "What is your degree"
chunk_text: - Write your academic level and the CIP code found on your I-20.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > "What is your employer's name as listed in E-Verify?
chunk_text: - Be sure to put the employer's name as listed in E-Verify.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “What is your employer's E-Verify company identification number or a valid E-Verify client company identification number?”
chunk_text: - The E-Verify number is not the employer's EIN. The E-Verify number is generally 4-7 digits. Check with your employer if you're not sure of the number.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “What is your reason for applying?”
chunk_text: - Choose the option “Renewal of my permission to accept employment”

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “U.S. Mailing Address”
chunk_text: - Your U.S. mailing address on the I-765 should be valid for at least four - five months. If you do not have an address that will be valid for four - five months, you can enter the name and U.S. address of a friend or family member under “In Care Of Name (if any).” You may also list a U.S. Post Office address (PO Box) if that is how you receive your mail.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “Status at last arrival”
chunk_text: - Scroll down to “F1 - F1 - Student, Academic Or Language Program.”

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “What is your A-Number?”
chunk_text: - This number is listed on your most recent EAD card, under “USCIS #.”

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “What is your USCIS Account Number?”
chunk_text: - Enter your USCIS account number if you have it. If not, click the box “I do not have or know my USCIS Online Account Number”.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > "Passport Photos"
chunk_text: - Submit two passport-style recent photos (within 30 days) that have not been previously used on other documents.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “I-94 Arrival and Departure Record”
chunk_text:
- Copy of your most recent
[I-94 arrival](https://i94.cbp.dhs.gov/I94/#/recent-search)record (not your travel history). Make sure the record indicates “Class of Admission: F1" and “Admit Until Date: D/S,” which stands for duration of status.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “Evidence: College Degree”
chunk_text: - A diploma AND an official transcript which show your degree and the date it was awarded.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “Evidence: Institutional Accreditation”
chunk_text:
- This is required
**only**if you are applying for a STEM OPT Extension based on a**prior degree**(i.e. not the OPT degree)

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “Application Fee”
chunk_text: - Pay the required application fee. Remember that once you pay the fee, there can be no changes to your application as it will be considered submitted.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Submit your STEM OPT I-765 application online through your USCIS account. > “Review and Submit”
chunk_text:
- Check that that you are within the 60-day window after the date your STEM OPT Extension I-20 was issued and before the expiration of your OPT EAD card. Do not submit if it is outside this timeframe, and contact the ISSO for assistance.

- Verify that all information is correct and digitally sign and submit your application.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Submit Your STEM OPT Application > 1Follow USCIS and USPS guidelines for tracking your EAD delivery
chunk_text:
Follow USCIS guidelines for [tracking EAD card delivery](https://www.uscis.gov/forms/filing-guidance/how-to-track-delivery-of-your-green-card-employment-authorization-document-ead-and-travel-document). Monitor your application status via your USCIS account and sign up for [USPS informed delivery](https://informeddelivery.usps.com/box/pages/intro/start.action) to track the delivery of your documents.

**Normally, you will receive 2 updates from USCIS before the EAD. **You do not need to contact us when you receive these two updates. If you receive other communication that requires follow up, contact the ISSO for assistance.

1. I-797 Receipt Notice with a case number. You will receive a case number immediately after submitting the online application. Check for accuracy in case there’s an error in your name or other information that needs to be corrected.

2. I-797 Approval Notice

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Submit Your STEM OPT Application > 2Continue working while your application is pending
chunk_text:
If you filed an application on time for a STEM extension, you will be able to continue employment while the extension application is pending, until a final decision is made or for 180 days, whichever comes first. Your employer may refer to section 7.4.2 of the [I-9 Handbook for Employers](https://www.uscis.gov/i-9-central/642-f-1-and-m-1-nonimmigrant-students) under F-1 STEM OPT Extension.

*If you are changing employers and your application is still pending, please [contact ISSO](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/contact).

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Submit Your STEM OPT Application > 3What to do If USCIS update indicates the EAD was delivered but you didn't receive it
chunk_text:
Try one of these two methods:

- Use USPS’
[Missing Mail and Lost Package Form](https://www.usps.com/help/missing-mail.htm)to locate your envelope.

- Visit your local US Post Office and ask them for detailed scanning information about where and when your package was scanned.

If USPS lost the EAD, attach a letter from the US Postal Service on official letterhead, stating that the USPS is responsible for the lost EAD, including the contact person from the US Post Office to send an email to the ISSO.

- Morningside: isso@columbia.edu
- CUIMC: cumc-intl-students@columbia.edu

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Submit Your STEM OPT Application > 4What if I receive a biometrics request from USCIS?
chunk_text: Applicants filing Form I-765 for employment authorization such as OPT and STEM OPT may receive a notice requiring them to attend a Biometrics Services Appointment at an [ASC](https://www.uscis.gov/about-us/find-a-uscis-office/application-support-centers). The notice with the appointment date and time will be uploaded to your USCIS account. Communication from USCIS regarding your application may also come to you via email or physical mail,mailed to the address listed on the I-765 application. **Frequently monitor your USCIS account for communication that requires follow up.**

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Submit Your STEM OPT Application > 5How to prepare for a biometrics appointment?
chunk_text: Please carefully review your USCIS notice for instructions, location, date and time and documents to bring with you to the appointment. For additional information, please visit [USCIS ](https://www.uscis.gov/forms/filing-guidance/preparing-for-your-biometric-services-appointment)website.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Submit Your STEM OPT Application > 6What happens after a biometrics appointment?
chunk_text: Check your [USCIS Account](https://my.uscis.gov/) for any updates after your visit.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Receive the STEM OPT EAD > STEM OPT Reporting Requirements
chunk_text: You and your employer have specific STEM OPT reporting obligations throughout your STEM OPT training period. For details, see our webpage on how to [comply with STEM OPT Reporting Requirements](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/stem-opt-reporting) every six months.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Receive the STEM OPT EAD > Changing or Adding Employers
chunk_text:
It is possible to change employers during the STEM OPT extension, or add a second concurrent employer. The same STEM OPT Extension requirements apply for each employment situation.

| ✓ | The new (or additional) employer must be enrolled in E-Verify before you begin to work. |
| ✓ | Your employment must be paid for at least 20 hours/week. |
| ✓ | You and the new employer must complete a new form I-983 Training Plan for STEM OPT Students and upload it in Compass within 10 days of beginning the new STEM OPT employment. Follow the instructions on theSTEM OPT Reporting page. |

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Receive the STEM OPT EAD > Traveling During the STEM OPT Extension
chunk_text: Traveling on OPT STEM Extension? Visit our [Travel page](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/travel-during-f-1-opt) for the documentation you need to return to the U.S. Do not travel until you have the STEM OPT EAD.

title: F-1 STEM OPT Extension | ISSO
section: After You Receive the STEM OPT EAD > Expiration of Your EAD
chunk_text:
When your EAD expires, you have a 60 day grace period in which:

| ✓ | to remain in the U.S. to prepare for departure or travel domestically, or |
| ✓ | transfer to another school, or |
| ✓ | change your visa status |

You cannot work during the grace period, or leave and return to the U.S. in F-1 status. On the 61st day after the EAD expires, SEVIS automatically completes your F-1 SEVIS record.

If you decide to discontinue your STEM OPT before the EAD expires, you do not have a grace period and must report your departure through ** STEM OPT Reporting. ** This will complete your F-1 SEVIS record.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: BEFORE YOU REPORT
chunk_text: Now that your [OPT](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/f-1-opt-optional-practical-training-after-your-degree-program) has been approved, keep the following things in mind.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: BEFORE YOU REPORT > You are limited to a total of 90 days without employment
chunk_text: You begin to count days without employment from the start date on your EAD. It will include any days before the start date of your employment and any days between jobs.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: BEFORE YOU REPORT > All OPT employment must be directly related to your field of study
chunk_text:
Employment on Post-Completion OPT is paid or unpaid work in your field of study for at least 20 hours per week.

When reporting employment, you will need to explain how this job directly relates to your Columbia major / degree.

Example: *I work as a** job title** for **employer name.** My duties include **list your duties**. At this job, I use **list relevant skills/knowledge from your coursework**, which directly relates to my **Columbia degree name.*

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: BEFORE YOU REPORT > Types of employment allowed
chunk_text:
- Type: Paid employment | Description: You must work at least 20 hours per week when on post-completion OPT.
- Type: Unpaid employment | Description: Unpaid work must be at least 20 hours per week and the work cannot violate any labor laws. Learn more about this on ourunpaid internships and volunteering webpageBe prepared to provide evidence from the employer that you worked at least 20 hours per week during the period of employment.Please note:STEM OPT extensionrequires paid employment
- Type: Remote employment | Description: OPT is work authorization in the U.S. for a U.S. based employer. That being said, we have seen U.S. employers allow or require for F-1 OPT students to work remotely. If traveling internationally while working remotely, entry into the US is always up to Customs and Border Protection. As such, ensure you consider that the longer you are outside the U.S., the more scrutinized your entry may potentially be.
- Type: Multiple employers | Description: You may work for more than one employer, concurrently or one at a time. All employment must be related to your degree program.
- Type: Short-term multiple employers | Description: Musicians and other performing artists should maintain a list of all "gigs", the dates and duration. If requested by DHS, you must be prepared to provide evidence showing a list of all gigs.
- Type: Employment through an agency or consulting firm | Description: Also known as employment, temp, or staffing agencies. You are required to average at least 20 hours/week at the location where you are placed.
- Type: Self-employed business owner | Description: You must be able to prove that you have the proper business license and that the business is related to your degree program. You may need to consult an attorney.
- Type: Independent contractor/ free-lance | Description: If requested by DHS, be prepared to provide evidence showing the duration of the contract periods and the name and address of the contracting company.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During Post-Completion OPT
chunk_text: You must report the following OPT employment updates and other changes no later than 10 days after the change.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During Post-Completion OPT > 1This is my first employment under OPT
chunk_text:
If you obtain employment, this is the first report to use. You will be asked to upload a copy of your OPT EAD card.

The start date you report is the date you start working (not the date you receive or accept an offer of employment).

When asked for your employer name, please indicate the name of the company or organization that you work for (not the name of your supervisor or manager).

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During Post-Completion OPT > 2I have a new employer
chunk_text: Use this report if you are ending employment with a previous employer **and **have a new employer. You will be asked for the end date of your previous employment and the start date of your new employment.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During Post-Completion OPT > 3My employer's name or address has changed
chunk_text: Use this report if you have already reported employment however your employer’s name or address has changed.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During Post-Completion OPT > 4I have an additional employer
chunk_text: Use this report if you have already reported your first employment and are now adding or starting new employment.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During Post-Completion OPT > 5I am no longer employed
chunk_text: Use this report if you are ending employment with a specific employer. You will be asked for the employer name and employment end date.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During Post-Completion OPT > 6I have departed the U.S. and will not use/resume OPT
chunk_text:
Use this report to notify the ISSO that you have departed the U.S. and will not use or resume OPT. The ISSO will permanently complete your F-1 SEVIS record.

Your Columbia I-20 can no longer be used to re-enter the US. Your EAD card will no longer be valid for employment. Your current F-1 SEVIS record will no longer be valid to [transfer to another school](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/transferring-your-f-1-sevis-record-another-school) or [begin a new program](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/extending-begin-new-program-f-j-students). You may obtain a new I-20 via a new SEVIS record should you gain admission to an F-1 eligible program in the future. If applicable, any F-2 dependent record(s) will also be completed.* *

Only submit the* "I have departed the U.S. and will not use/resume OPT" *report after you have departed the U.S.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During Post-Completion OPT > 7I now hold a new immigration status in the U.S.
chunk_text: Use this report to notify the ISSO about your new approved status. The ISSO will permanently complete your F-1 SEVIS record. You will be asked for proof of your new immigration status.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: How to Report During Post-Completion OPT
chunk_text: Log in to Compass to submit your updates and changes while on Post-Completion OPT.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: After You Report > 1The SEVP Portal is optional
chunk_text: The SEVP Portal allows you to review your SEVIS record. You are required to report all employment updates to the ISSO. The ISSO will update your SEVIS record. You can confirm your OPT employment updates through the SEVP Portal.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO

chunk_text: The ISSO does not automatically provide an I-20 after OPT is approved or after OPT employment reports are submitted. chunk_text: Keep a record of all your employment. For each job, maintain evidence of the job title, dates of employment, contact information for your supervisor, and a description of the work and how it is related to your program of study. It is useful to have these personal records available in case any questions arise during OPT or in a future employment-based application, such as H-1B.

title: OPT Reporting | ISSO

chunk_text: If a change of your US address is the only update you need to report, go to the[ Change of Address](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/changing-your-address) page.

chunk_text:

chunk_text: Now that your [STEM OPT Extension](https://isso.columbia.edu/content/f-1-stem-opt-extension) has been approved, keep the following things in mind:

title: STEM OPT Reporting | ISSO

chunk_text:
The 6-, 12-, 18-, and 24-month STEM OPT validation reports are based on the start date of your STEM OPT Extension's EAD card.

The earliest we can accept the 6-, 12-, 18-, and 24-month STEM OPT validation reports is 30 days before they are due.

The latest we can update your F-1 SEVIS record and SEVP Portal information is up to 30 days after they are due.

chunk_text: In addition to STEM OPT validation reports, you need to report any change to your name, address, place of employment, or end of employment within 10 days.

title: STEM OPT Reporting | ISSO

chunk_text: You are limited to a total of 150 days without employment during the period encompassing both Post-Completion OPT and the STEM OPT extension.

title: STEM OPT Reporting | ISSO

chunk_text:
All STEM OPT employers must be enrolled in [E-Verify](https://www.e-verify.gov/).

A complete and signed [I-983 STEM OPT Training Plan](https://www.ice.gov/doclib/sevis/pdf/i983.pdf) is required for all STEM OPT employment.

chunk_text:

You will also be asked to provide a copy of your STEM OPT EAD card.

title: STEM OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During STEM OPT > 2Submit your 12-month validation report
chunk_text:
12 months *after *your STEM OPT EAD card’s start date, use Compass to report your current U.S. address.

You and your employer will also need to complete your Evaluation of Student Progress.

The Evaluation of Student Progress can be found on the** top half of page 5** of the

[I-983 STEM OPT Training Plan](https://www.ice.gov/doclib/sevis/pdf/i983.pdf).

title: STEM OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: Reporting Requirements During STEM OPT > 3Submit your 18-month Validation report
chunk_text: 18 months *after *your STEM OPT EAD card’s start date, use Compass to report your current U.S. address and confirm your employer information.

chunk_text:

The Final Evaluation can be found on the** bottom half of page 5** of the

Use this report if you are ending employment with a previous employer ** and **have a new employer.

chunk_text:
Use this report to notify the ISSO that you have departed the U.S. and will not use or resume STEM OPT. The ISSO will permanently complete your F-1 SEVIS record.

You may obtain a new I-20 via a new SEVIS record should you gain admission to an F-1 eligible program in the future. If applicable, any F-2 dependent record(s) will also be completed.* *

title: STEM OPT Reporting | ISSO
section: The SEVP Portal and STEM OPT
chunk_text:
You cannot submit your STEM OPT employment changes and validation reports using the SEVP Portal. You must submit them to the ISSO using Compass. The ISSO will then update your SEVIS record, which will be reflected in your SEVP Portal.

You will receive notifications from SEVP regarding your 6-, 12-, 18-, and 24-month STEM OPT validation report requirements. The ISSO can update your SEVIS record up to 30 days after they are due. It is your responsibility to submit your reports in a timely manner.

If you have any questions regarding SEVP emails related to your STEM OPT, you may contact SEVP directly via their contact information [here](https://studyinthestates.dhs.gov/sevp-portal-help).

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > Eligibility
chunk_text:
You are eligible for an extension of both your F-1 status and OPT if:

- ✓
- Your employer files an H-1B petition as a change of status, not as a consular processing, with USCIS with an employment start date of October 1.

- ✓
- You are on authorized OPT or STEM OPT extension on the date USCIS receives the petition.

- ✓
- You have a pending or approved change of status to H-1B. Selection in the H-1B electronic registration lottery or a Registration Selection Notice is not sufficient documentation. You will need your I-797C Receipt Notice OR I-797A Approval Notice.
- "Cap-gap" work authorization ends on September 30th, even if you have an H-1B petition that remains pending on October 1st.

| ✓ | Your employer files an H-1B petition as a change of status, not as a consular processing, with USCIS with an employment start date of October 1. |
| ✓ | You are on authorized OPT or STEM OPT extension on the date USCIS receives the petition. |
| ✓ | You have a pending or approved change of status to H-1B. Selection in the H-1B electronic registration lottery or a Registration Selection Notice is not sufficient documentation. You will need your I-797C Receipt Notice OR I-797A Approval Notice."Cap-gap" work authorization ends on September 30th, even if you have an H-1B petition that remains pending on October 1st. |

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > Before You Apply > What is cap-gap extension of OPT?
chunk_text:
The **cap** refers to the limit or quota on the number of individuals who can receive H-1B status at the beginning of the government's fiscal year — October 1st. The cap is currently set at 65,000 recipients plus an additional 20,000 for those with US Master's degree or higher.

The **gap** is the period of time between the end of your F-1 OPT and the beginning of H-1B status. If you graduated in May, typically your 12 months of OPT will end sometime between mid-May and mid-July, leaving a gap in legal status and authorized employment through September 30th.

The **Cap-Gap rule** automatically extends F-1 status and, when applicable, extends OPT work authorization:

**Cap-Gap**until September 30th if you have your H-1B- I-797 receipt from USCIS
**or** - I-797 Approval Notice

- I-797 receipt from USCIS

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > Before You Apply > The cap-gap extension of OPT through September 30 is automatic if you’re eligible
chunk_text:
If eligible, you don’t apply for a new EAD and don't pay a new USCIS fee. Your employer can allow you to continue to work on the basis of the timely-filing of your H-1B petition while your OPT was valid.

An updated “cap-gap” I-20 will reflect your extended OPT permission to work until September 30th if the H-1B petition has been receipted or approved.

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > Before You Apply > Cap-gap employment authorization ends on September 30th
chunk_text: “Cap-gap” work authorization is valid only through September 30th, even if you have an H-1B petition that remains pending on Oct. 1. If you continue to work without authorization, you risk accruing [unlawful presence](http://links.govdelivery.com/track?type=click&enid=ZWFzPTEmbWFpbGluZ2lkPTIwMTgwOTI4Ljk1NTQyNTYxJm1lc3NhZ2VpZD1NREItUFJELUJVTC0yMDE4MDkyOC45NTU0MjU2MSZkYXRhYmFzZWlkPTEwMDEmc2VyaWFsPTE3NTUwOTUzJmVtYWlsaWQ9Um1kMjIwMkBjb2x1bWJpYS5lZHUmdXNlcmlkPVJtZDIyMDJAY29sdW1iaWEuZWR1JmZsPSZleHRyYT1NdWx0aXZhcmlhdGVJZD0mJiY=&&&100&&&https://www.uscis.gov/legal-resources/unlawful-presence-and-bars-admissibility).

chunk_text:
The earliest a “cap subject” employer can file an H-1 petition on your behalf is 6 months in advance of the requested start date, which means April 1 for the following October 1.

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > Travel During Cap-Gap Extension Period > Do not leave the US while the H-1B change of status is pending
chunk_text: It will nullify the change of status and make you ineligible for the cap gap extension.

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > Travel During Cap-Gap Extension Period > Travel Abroad After H-1B is Approved
chunk_text:
According to [USCIS information on the Cap-Gap extension](https://www.uscis.gov/working-united-states/temporary-workers/h-1b-specialty-occupations-and-fashion-models/extension-post-completion-optional-practical-training-opt-and-f-1-status-eligible-students-under-h-1b-cap-gap-regulations), generally, you may travel abroad and return to the US in F-1 status during a Cap-Gap period if:

- ✓
- Your change of status to H-1B has been approved

- ✓
- You return before your H-1B employment begins (normally at the beginning of the government's fiscal year—October 1)

- ✓
- You have other documents required for F-1 readmission (unexpired passport, unexpired F-1 entry visa)

| ✓ | Your change of status to H-1B has been approved |
| ✓ | You return before your H-1B employment begins (normally at the beginning of the government's fiscal year—October 1) |
| ✓ | You have other documents required for F-1 readmission (unexpired passport, unexpired F-1 entry visa) |

Please be sure to consult with your employer and/or attorney regarding [USCIS travel guidelines](https://www.uscis.gov/working-in-the-united-states/temporary-workers/h-1b-specialty-occupations-and-fashion-models/extension-of-post-completion-optional-practical-training-opt-and-f-1-status-for-eligible-students) as it relates to your H1B application.

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > FAQs (Frequently Asked Questions) > What if my OPT expires before April 1st and I am in my 60-day grace period when my employer files the H-1 petition?
chunk_text: Your F-1 legal status to remain in the US may be extended, but unfortunately you won't have permission to continue working.

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > FAQs (Frequently Asked Questions) > How does my employer verify my employment eligibility on an I-9 after my EAD has expired?
chunk_text: You and your employer will need your passport, EAD card, I-797 receipt notice for H-1B Petition or I-797 B approval notice, Cap Gap I-20, passport. They can refer to page 21 of the [Employers’ I-9 Manual.](https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/files/form/m-274.pdf)

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > FAQs (Frequently Asked Questions) > What happens if my H-1B petition is subsequently rejected, denied or revoked after I become eligible for an automatic Cap-Gap Extension?
chunk_text:
If your H-1B petition is not selected and approved, you will have the standard 60-day grace period from the date of the rejection notice to depart the US, transfer the I-20 to a new program, or apply for a change of status.

However, if a denial is based on a discovered status violation, there is no grace period and you have to leave the US immediately.

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > FAQs (Frequently Asked Questions) > Do the limits on unemployment time apply with a Cap-Gap Extension?
chunk_text: Yes. The 90-day limitation on unemployment during the initial post-completion 12-Month OPT authorization continues during the cap-gap extension, as does the additional 60-day limitation on unemployment under the STEM OPT extension guidelines.

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > FAQs (Frequently Asked Questions) > May I apply for a STEM OPT Extension during my Cap-Gap OPT Extension?
chunk_text: Yes, but your STEM OPT Extension application must be received by USCIS before your cap-gap extension period expires.

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > FAQs (Frequently Asked Questions) > Does my dependent’s status also get extended with my Cap-Gap extension?
chunk_text: Yes. Your F-2 dependent’s status is also extended.

title: Cap-Gap OPT Extension | ISSO
section: Cap-Gap OPT Extension > Want to learn more?
chunk_text: See what USCIS says about the [Cap-Gap Extension.](https://www.uscis.gov/working-united-states/temporary-workers/h-1b-specialty-occupations-and-fashion-models/extension-post-completion-optional-practical-training-opt-and-f-1-status-eligible-students-under-h-1b-cap-gap-regulations)

title: Entry to the U.S.: Travel and Re-Entry | Berkeley International Office
section: Entry to the U.S.: Travel and Re-Entry | Berkeley International Office
chunk_text:
Required Documents

**** BIO RECOMMENDS ENTERING THE U.S. VIA THE SAN FRANCISCO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT (SFO) PORT OF ENTRY WHENEVER POSSIBLE. ****

Documentation needed for travel may vary depending on the relationship you hold with UC Berkeley. Please refer to the below definitions to ensure you understand which term applies to you.

**F-1/J-1 students:** F-1 and J-1 students enrolled in a UC Berkeley degree-seeking or UCEAP program, or on post-completion OPT, STEM OPT, or Academic Training

**J-1 scholars:** J-1 scholars hold formal affiliations at UC Berkeley to conduct research or teach; J-1 scholars are NOT enrolled in UC Berkeley degree-seeking or UCEAP programs.

**When traveling, carry documents with you in carry-on luggage, NOT in your checked baggage! You need access to your visa documents at all times during transit. **

**Passport**

Your passport must be valid at least six months into the future at all times, especially upon initial entry or re-entry to the United States. If yours will expire soon, contact your [home country's embassy](https://travel.state.gov/content/travel/en/consularnotification/ConsularNotificationandAccess.html) in the United States.

**U.S. Entry Visa**

The visa stamp in your passport should be valid **on the date of your return** to the United States. (It does not need to remain valid during your stay in the United States). If you have an old, expired passport with a valid U.S. entry visa, you do not need to get a new visa stamp in your new passport as long as you carry both passports when you enter the United States. Canadian citizens do not need a U.S. entry visa. Canadian "Landed immigrants" (Permanent Residents, without Candian citizenship/passport) are required to obtain U.S. entry visas. For more information about visas, see [Understanding Your Visa.](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/us-entry-visa)

**F-1 students**- the name of the school on the Form I-20 should match the name of the school on the visa. The only exception is for students who directly [transferred your I-20 ](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/transfer-uc-berkeley)to UC Berkeley using the same SEVIS ID. See our [Transfer Students](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/transfer-uc-berkeley) page for more information. If your previous visa was from a school other than UC Berkeley, and you are beginning a new SEVIS record (without SEVIS transfer), BIO recommends that you [obtain a new visa](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/visa_application) at a U.S. Embassy or Consulate abroad, if this is possible before your re-entry. **J-1 Exchange Visitors** should only enter the United States with a visa showing the school's (or program) name noted on it, even if the visa has not yet expired. If you change schools, obtain a visa with the new name noted on it before entering or re-entering the United States.

In certain cases, visitors may be able to re-enter the United States with an expired visa after a short trip to Canada/Mexico using [Automatic Visa Revalidation](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/travel-canada-and-mexico).

See [Applying for a Visa](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/visa-application) for more information about applying for a new F or J visa.

**Evidence of Financial Support**

If you change schools, obtain a visa with the new name noted on it before entering or re-entering the United States.

In certain cases, visitors may be able to re-enter the United States with an expired visa after a short trip to Canada/Mexico using [Automatic Visa Revalidation](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/travel-canada-and-mexico).

See [Applying for a Visa](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/visa-application) for more information about applying for a new F or J visa.

**Evidence of Financial Support**

It is strongly recommended that F/J nonimmigrants carry proof of the financial support and resources reflected on the Form I-20 or DS-2019. Proof of financial support bank statements, or financial support letters should be dated within the last 6 months.

**I-20 or DS-2019 (F-1/F-2, J-1/J-2 documents)**

Make sure your original form [I-20](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/understanding-your-i-20) (F-1) or [DS-2019](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/ds-2019) (J-1) has been PROPERLY ENDORSED by the school official or agency that issued the document. For **current F/J Students and Scholars**, an I-20 or DS-2019 travel endorsement is valid for **12 months**, or *until the expiration of the document*, whichever is first. Please note: Those on **F-1 post-completion OPT/STEM OPT** must have the endorsement within the past **6 months.**

**F-1 or J-1 Students:**See here for how to obtain a[Travel Endorsement (travel signature)](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/students/student-services/travel-endorsement-travel-signatures)on your F-1 I-20 or J-1 DS-2019.**J-1 Visiting Scholars, Professors, Researchers and Student Researchers:**To schedule an appointment to come to BIO for a travel signature, please email[jscholar@berkeley.edu](mailto:jscholar@berkeley.edu)and a link to schedule an appointment will be provided. Alternatively, you can email a[Travel Signature Request Form](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/sites/default/files/tsrf_jun_2024.pdf)with copies of your most recent DS-2019 and J-1 visa to[jscholar@berkeley.edu](mailto:jscholar@berkeley.edu)to request an updated DS-2019 with a new travel signature.**F-1 Students on post-completion OPT/STEM OPT**see also[OPT Travel Information](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/students/training/f-1/travel).

**SEVIS (I-901) Fee Payment**

It is also recommended to travel with proof of your [SEVIS fee](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/sevis-fee) payment. If you need confirmation of your previous SEVIS fee payment, visit the [SEVIS fee payment page](https://www.fmjfee.com/) and select the option to "Check I-901 Status/ Print Payment Confirmation."

**Proof of Admission/Enrollment (New/Current F/J enrolled Students) or Proof of UC Berkeley Appointment (J Scholars)**

**F or J enrolled students** (degree-seeking or UCEAP students): **New students **(entering for the first time) should travel with proof of admission. **Continuing students** should travel with proof of the current or next semester's registration (e.g. printout of full-time class enrollment or Enrollment Verification from [Cal Student Central](https://calcentral.berkeley.edu/)).

title: Entry to the U.S.: Travel and Re-Entry | Berkeley International Office
section: How Can I Prepare for Re-Entry and Understand My Rights at the Border?
chunk_text:
**** BIO RECOMMENDS ENTERING THE U.S. VIA THE SAN FRANCISCO INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT (SFO) PORT OF ENTRY WHENEVER POSSIBLE. ****

**Given the current U.S. immigration climate, you should be prepared for the possibility of increased scrutiny at the U.S. port of entry in the form of additional questioning and/or inspection of your phone or social media. Be aware: Officers may ask you questions about your reason for entering the U.S., your activities in the U.S., or your academic program. **

-
If you are questioned, remain calm and answer honestly and succinctly. What NOT to say: Jokes about terrorism, explosives, breaking U.S. laws, drugs, alcohol, etc. are never a good idea.

-
Preparing Your Electronic Devices: CBP may also ask questions about your electronic devices or inspect them. This may include your social media accounts. For tips on how to prepare your devices, read this

[ACLU article](https://www.aclu.org/blog/privacy-technology/privacy-borders-and-checkpoints/can-border-agents-search-your-electronic?redirect=blog/free-future/can-border-agents-search-your-electronic-devices-its-complicated). -
Social Media & App Review: Are you presenting your best self in social media and messaging apps? Take time to review your accounts for any content that may appear to show you engaged in activities that violate U.S. laws, your visa status, or academic integrity.

- Know Your Rights: See the
[ACLU's resources](https://www.aclu.org/know-your-rights/immigrants-rights)for information on your[rights at airports/borders](https://www.aclu.org/know-your-rights/what-do-when-encountering-law-enforcement-airports-and-other-ports-entry-us)and in[encounters with law enforcement](https://www.aclu.org/know-your-rights/immigrants-rights#law-enforcement-asks-about-my-immigration-status).

If there are any problems during entry, please notify Berkeley International Office as soon as you arrive.

title: On-Campus Employment | Berkeley International Office
section: What is On-Campus Employment?
chunk_text:
On-campus employment must meet one of the following definitions:

- The employment takes place on school premises and the employee (student) is paid by UC Berkeley for the work. Examples include GSI/GSR positions or jobs at campus facilities or departments. This is the most common type of on-campus employment.
- The employment takes place at a commercial firm (e.g., bookstore, coffee shop) that is located on the UC Berkeley campus and provides services for students on campus.
- The employment takes place at an off-campus location that is educationally affiliated with UC Berkeley. The affiliation must be associated with the school's established curriculum or related to contractually-funded research projects at the post-graduate level. The employment must be an integral part of the student's educational program.

Please consult with the Berkeley International Office if you need help determining whether your employment qualifies as on-campus.

title: On-Campus Employment | Berkeley International Office
section: How do I find a job on campus as an international student?
chunk_text:
The Berkeley Career Engagement Center allows you to [search for on-campus jobs via Handshake](https://berkeley.joinhandshake.com/stu/postings?page=1&per_page=25&sort_direction=desc&sort_column=default&job.job_types%5B%5D=6), or visit their web page for [on-campus opportunities](https://career.berkeley.edu/find-opportunities/experiential-learning/campus-opportunities/).

Note that many positions are limited to U.S. nationals because they require the federally supported "work-study designation." However, with a little effort and networking, you can find something on campus. Many offices and departments on campus hire student workers and not all are posted online. In some cases on-campus jobs are found by asking around, being referred by other students, or getting involved in student clubs and organizations.

Good luck with your search!

title: On-Campus Employment | Berkeley International Office
section: Am I eligible for "work-study" jobs?
chunk_text: Unfortunately, F-1 and J-1 international students are not eligible to accept "work-study" jobs. Work-study salaries are paid with U.S. federal funds, therefore F-1 and J-1 students cannot receive this type of funding.

title: On-Campus Employment | Berkeley International Office
section: Do I need a Social Security Number?
chunk_text: Yes, all F-1 and J-1 students who will be employed will need to apply for a Social Security Number (SSN). You will apply after you get the job offer and visit BIO. For more information about this process, please see [Social Security Numbers](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/living/ssn).

title: On-Campus Employment | Berkeley International Office
section: Do I need a Social Security Number? > I am an F-1 student. Do I need permission to work on campus?
chunk_text: **No.** F-1 students with UC Berkeley-issued I-20s may work on-campus without any extra authorization while they are fully-enrolled students with valid, unexpired I-20s.

title: On-Campus Employment | Berkeley International Office
section: Do I need a Social Security Number? > F-1 On-Campus Employment Rules
chunk_text:
- In the fall and spring semesters as well as the last semester, students are limited to 20 hours per week of on-campus work.
- In the summer between spring/fall semesters, students may work more than 20 hours per week. However, if summer is the
*first*or*final*semester, employment is limited to 20 hours per week. - F-1 on-campus employment authorization ends at the expiration of the I-20 OR the end of the student's final semester of enrollment, whichever occurs first.
- F-1 students will need to apply for a
[Social Security Number](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/social-security)for all paid on-campus work.

title: On-Campus Employment | Berkeley International Office
section: Do I need a Social Security Number? > I am a J-1 student. Do I need permission to work on campus?
chunk_text:
**Yes.** J-1 students must obtain written approval from their program sponsor (BIO) before beginning any type of on-campus employment.

**Authorization is employer-specific.** If there is a change in the employment (including position, hiring unit, or number of hours per week), a new authorization is required. Authorizations are granted only 1 year at a time.

**Steps to obtain authorization:**

- Request authorization at least 5 business days before beginning on campus employment by submitting the completed
[J-1 On-Campus Employment Authorization Form](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/sites/default/files/j_on-campus_employment.docx)to Berkeley International Office. - A BIO adviser will process the employment authorization in the Student and Exchange Visitor System (SEVIS) and sign the On-Campus Employment Authorization Form. BIO email you the signed form in 5 business days.
[Apply for a Social Security Number](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/living/ssnapply)(SSN) using the signed J-1 Employment/Fellowship Authorization form.

title: On-Campus Employment | Berkeley International Office
section: Do I need a Social Security Number? > J-1 On-campus Employment Rules
chunk_text:
- To complete the hiring process, students must show the completed form, signed by a BIO adviser, to the UC Berkeley hiring unit.
- In the fall and spring semesters as well as the last semester, students are limited to 20 hours per week of on-campus work.
- In the summer between spring/fall semesters, students may work more than 20 hours per week. However, if summer is the
*first*or*final*semester, employment is limited to 20 hours per week. - The on-campus employment authorization form is invalid if the student graduates, fails to maintain J-1 status, or transfers out of UC Berkeley.
- Employment authorization is valid for a maximum of one year per site of employment. Students should check the approved Authorization section of the form to determine the length of authorization and number of hours per week.
- To renew on-campus employment authorization or report changes, please resubmit the form with the updated information. Students must obtain a new work authorization form when there is a change in the number of hours per week.
- J-1 students will need to apply for a
[Social Security Number](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/social-security)for all paid on-campus work.

title: F-1 Curricular Practical Training (CPT) | Berkeley International Office
section: What is Curricular Practical Training (CPT)?
chunk_text:
Curricular Practical Training (CPT) is a type of academically related work-based training authorization for F-1 students to gain pratical experience in their field of study. CPT is used during the degree program and is not available after degree program.

**OPT Ineligility & High CPT Use**- Use of 12 months or more of full-time CPT at the same degree level will make an F-1 student ineligible for post-completion[Optional Practical Training](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/students/employment/opt). Use of part-time CPT does not negatively affect eligibility for OPT, but high CPT usage may cause DHS to subject records to increased scrutiny when the student applies for OPT, visa applications, or other DHS benefits like H-1B petitions. UC Berkeley has noted a significant increase in Requests for Evidence for STEM OPT applications and H-1Bs for students with high rates of CPT usage.**CPT is Academic**- CPT is an academic experience which is integral to a stduent's degree program, and is not intended to be used for regular on-going off-campus work. Students should be mindful to keep all records of CPT and the academic work related to CPT, and how that employment is integral to the degree program. Students will need to retain copies of all previous CPT I-20s when applying later for OPT.**CPT can be paid or unpaid**- Any off-campus work experience should be authorized, whether paid or unpaid.

**Eligibility for CPT**

CPT eligiblity requires that F-1 students:

**Be in valid F-1 immigration status for**before becoming eligible for CPT.*at least two full-time semesters*(1 academic year)**Exceptions to 1 Academic Year Requirement (Rare):**1) Graduate students who have internship as a degree requirement for all students which must occur prior to completion of 1 academic year. 2) Students who have changed from some other US visa status to F-1 student status and have been continually enrolled full-time for an academic year during the change of status period.**Enroll for Internship/Field Study/Practicum Course Credit OR be completing a Degree Requirement.**(See below for Degree Requirement or Course Credit CPT.)**Secure an academically appropriate internship offer directly related to the student's current degree**. NOTE: Positions must be appropriate to the student's level of study and major field of study. Retail sales, food service, and other unskilled positions will be denied.**Apply for and Receive CPT authorization BEFORE beginning employment**. Authorization cannot be back-dated.**Be registered for classes at UC Berkeley**; CPT cannot be used when a graduate student is on Filing Fee status. Enrollment in internship coursework may be required.**Be Enrolled in a Full Course Load or have**- Students are not eligible for CPT in the same semester as they have been authorized for a[Final Term RCL](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/students/current/full-time_reqs#:~:text=electronically%20(MEDICAL%20RCL)-,Final%20Term%20RCL,-Degree%2Dseeking%20students)[First Semester Academic Reduced Course Load](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/students/current/full-time_reqs#:~:text=L%26S%20undergrads-,Academic%20RCL,-THIS%20REASON%20IS)or[Medical Reduced Course Load](https://internationaloffice.berkeley.edu/students/current/full-time_reqs#:~:text=Medical%20RCLs%20and%20Medical%20Withdrawals). Students authorized for a Final Term RCL must also be completing additional required in-person degree requirement coursework, unless the final term CPT is the only remaining Degree Requirement.- A
**ppropriate supervision and oversight.**The student's supervisor for the internship cannot be an F-1 or J-1 student. BIO does not authorize CPT for self-employment.

title: F-1 Curricular Practical Training (CPT) | Berkeley International Office
section: What is Curricular Practical Training (CPT)? > Types of CPT
chunk_text:
**What is Degree Requirement CPT?**

Degree Requirement CPT may be authorized when an internship, practicum, or capstone work experience is a requirement of a degree program (i.e., all students in the program must complete an internship to obtain the degree). Authorization can be for full-time or part-time employment, depending on the academic department's requirement. Proof of course enrollment for this type of CPT is not required but degree requirement.

****Degree requirement CPT can only be used once (to fulfill the requirement) and should occur during the program’s normal internship period. ****

Currently, the following programs at UC Berkeley have been identified as requiring employment or practical training to graduate:

- Bachelor of Science in Educational Sciences (3 units EDUC W144, part-time only during Fall/Spring)
- Masters of Analytics
- Masters of Bioengineering/Masters of Translational Medicine (
*only*if needed*to*complete Capstone Project: Bioengineering 296) - Masters of Biotechology
- Masters of City Planning
- Masters of Design (MDes)
- Masters Development Engineering (MDE)
- Masters of Development Practice
- Masters of Financial Engineering- only during the dates of 1 designated internship period. See
[MFE calendar](https://mfe.haas.berkeley.edu/academics/calendar) - Masters of Journalism
- Masters of Landscape Architecture
- PhD in Metabolic Biology
- Master of Nutrition Science and Dietetics (MNSD)
- Master of Business Administration (MBA)
- Masters of Public Affairs
- Masters of Public Policy
- Masters of Public Health (MPH)
- Doctor of Public Health (DrPH)
- MRED+D
- Masters of Social Welfare
- Masters of Teacher Education (BTEP)
- Optometry Residency Program
- PhD in School Psychology

What is CPT for Course Credit?

[What is CPT for Course Credit?]

CPT may be authorized for students who enroll in a course that requires employment to earn a grade, or a course where students design their own research project based on the employment.

What is CPT for Course Credit?

[What is CPT for Course Credit?]

CPT may be authorized for students who enroll in a course that requires employment to earn a grade, or a course where students design their own research project based on the employment.

**Course enrollment must be in the student's major department (or intended major for undeclared students)**or via[Berkeley Global Internships](http://studyabroad.berkeley.edu/globalinternships)OR[Berkeley Career Engagement Center Independent U.S. Summer Internship](https://career.berkeley.edu/find-opportunities/internships/academic-credit-for-internships/).The student's academic department determines the appropriate number of internship units according to the academic program.**Students with multiple majors**should enroll for course credit in the major that is directly related to the CPT experience**Enrollment in the course must be concurrent with the employment**AND CPT employment must overlap with the internship enrollment semester dates. Employment may take place in the fall, spring, or summer terms.- Authorization is be granted on a semester-by-semester basis.
- You may request a CPT start date that either begins or ends during the semester break immediately before or after the internship semester. Students whose internship dates extend into the semester breaks will need to have a legitimate academic reason for requiring an CPT start/end outside of normal semester dates. (see
